summary: Iteration 7 frontend testing — Read-Only / Showcase Mode. MIXED result: top-level UI affordances (button label, banner, footer, disabled attrs on top-bar controls and preset modal) all work correctly, but the CORE inert-based protection that is supposed to lock SelectedChannelPanel, all ChannelStrips, and the MatrixRouter is BROKEN. The `inert` attribute is not being applied to the DOM by React 19 because the code passes an empty string (`inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}`), which React 19 treats as the boolean value `false` (with a runtime warning in console). As a result, while the app appears LOCKED, users can still: mute/solo any channel strip, toggle Pink Noise per channel, drag faders/pans/delays/crossover knobs, change EQ/Comp/Lim settings via the inline SelectedChannelPanel, AND toggle any of the 1024 matrix routing cells. The locking promise is materially broken for the most important surfaces.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL — `<div inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}>` does not apply inert to the DOM in React 19. Console emits: 'Received an empty string for a boolean attribute … This will treat the attribute as if it were false. … inert'. Verified via DOM read: getAttribute('inert') returns null while readOnly is true; clicking a channel strip mute (data-testid=`sel-mute`) DID toggle its background from transparent → red, proving state changed inside the supposedly-inert region.

Same React-19 boolean-attribute issue: the `<div inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined} className="overflow-x-auto grow">` wrapping the ChannelStrip rows also does not receive the inert attribute. All per-channel mute/solo/pinkNoise/crossover/EQ-band/dyn/delay/pan/fader controls remain interactive while LOCKED.
Same root cause — matrix cell `route-in_phy_0-out_phy_0` DID toggle (bg `rgb(255,107,0)` → empty) when clicked while LOCKED. Routing matrix is fully editable in lock mode.

integration_issues.issue: Half of the spec works (disabled-attr controls), but inert-wrapped regions are NOT actually locked. The yellow LOCKED banner misrepresents the actual state — users can still edit channels and routing.

action_items: FIX (one-line): change `inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}` in three places (DSPApp.jsx lines 22, 40, 116) to `inert={readOnly || undefined}` OR `inert={readOnly ? true : undefined}` so React 19 emits the attribute as a true HTML boolean. Verify by reading `document.querySelector('[data-testid="channels-edit-region"]').hasAttribute('inert')` returns true while readOnly is true. After the fix, re-test that channel-strip mute, EQ band drag, matrix-cell click, and SelectedChannelPanel knobs ALL no longer change state while LOCKED.
Alternative for older browsers: spread approach `{...(readOnly ? { inert: '' } : {})}` works because React only adds the attr when present, but in React 19 the cleaner idiom is to pass boolean true.

Consider adding a defensive belt-and-braces approach: gate write-actions in DspContext helpers (updateOutput / toggleRoute / etc.) with `if (readOnly) return;`. This protects against future regressions if inert is ever removed.
Console warning 'Received an empty string for a boolean attribute … inert' should disappear after the fix — verify it is gone.
critical_code_review_comments: Root cause: React 19 changed the handling of boolean attributes — empty string is now coerced to false (and warned). The current `inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}` therefore never actually applies inert. This is a single-character class of bug that defeats the entire feature for inert-wrapped regions.

Defense-in-depth gap: there is no programmatic check in the reducer/helpers (updateOutput, toggleRoute, setMaster, etc.). The lockdown relies 100% on UI-layer attribute correctness. If a developer adds a new control inside the inert region and forgets the disabled prop, OR the inert mechanism breaks (as it has here), the underlying state mutates freely.

TopBar disabled-attr handling is correct and ergonomic: version buttons, master-gain, master-mute, pn-master-toggle, pn-master-level all have `disabled={readOnly}` plus disabled-state styling (opacity-40, cursor-not-allowed) — these survive the React-19 issue because `disabled` IS still recognized when passed as boolean true.

PresetManager correctly disables save / import-file-input / delete and leaves load/export enabled (read-only operations). Import label's inner file input is correctly disabled via the actual <input disabled> rather than relying on the label's CSS — good.
ReadOnly state is intentionally NOT persisted to localStorage (only in useState in DspProvider line 100) — verified by reload: locked state reverts to EDITABLE. Per spec.

ChannelPills and tab buttons are correctly NOT wrapped in inert and remain interactive while locked — verified pill-out_phy_2 selects and pill-bank-virt/phy switch banks. Good navigation/edit separation.
Footer text correctly switches from 'State auto-saved to local storage' → '🔒 Read-Only — state preserved' based on readOnly flag. data-testid='footer-state-saved' wires up cleanly.
Banner (data-testid='readonly-banner') renders only when readOnly is true and uses a high-contrast yellow #FFD60A background with black text — accessible and obvious.
Lock-toggle button color/icon swap is well done: 🔓 EDITABLE (transparent, gray border) ↔ 🔒 LOCKED (yellow background, black text, yellow border). Clear affordance.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (8/12 PASS, 4 CRITICAL FAIL). Working: button toggle, banner show/hide, footer text, all TopBar `disabled` controls (versions/master-gain/master-mute/pn-master-*), tab+pill+bank navigation while locked, preset modal save/import/delete disabled while load enabled, unlock reverts everything, reload does not persist readOnly. BROKEN: channels-edit-region inert not applied; channel-strips wrapper inert not applied; matrix-router wrapper inert not applied; clicks inside these 'inert' regions still mutate state.

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None. The toggle was clicked, and a channel strip mute was toggled twice (mute then un-mute) during the failure verification — net-zero state change on persisted store.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Read-Only Mode testids: readonly-toggle (TopBar), readonly-banner (below TopBar when locked), footer-state-saved (footer right). DSPApp.jsx uses `inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}` at lines 22, 40, 116 — this is the bug because React 19 coerces empty string to false on boolean attrs. The fix is `inert={readOnly || undefined}` (or pass boolean true). The disabled-attr approach used elsewhere works because `disabled` accepts boolean true and React preserves it. After fixing, re-verify: (a) `document.querySelector('[data-testid="channels-edit-region"]').hasAttribute('inert') === true` while locked; (b) clicking sel-mute or any out-out_phy-N-mute does NOT toggle background color; (c) clicking any route-in_X-out_Y cell does NOT toggle its background; (d) the React warning 'Received an empty string for a boolean attribute … inert' is gone from console. Note: Playwright `page.click` does NOT bypass real `inert` in Chromium — when inert is properly applied, the onClick handler will not fire because the browser doesn't dispatch the event to descendants of an inert subtree. So state-comparison before/after click is the correct way to verify the fix.
rca of the issue: Bug: inert-based lockdown does not work. RCA: In React 19, boolean HTML attributes are stricter — passing `inert=""` (empty string) is coerced to the boolean value `false` and a runtime warning is emitted ('Received an empty string for a boolean attribute … inert'). The intent of the original idiom (which worked in React 18) was that an empty-string `inert` attribute is truthy in HTML. The browser-level inert spec does treat any presence of the attribute (including empty string) as inert, but React no longer SETS the attribute when the JSX value is `''`. Reproduction: (1) lock the app via readonly-toggle; (2) run `document.querySelector('[data-testid="channels-edit-region"]').hasAttribute('inert')` in DevTools → returns false; (3) click any sel-mute or matrix cell → background changes, state mutates. Mitigation: change the three `inert={readOnly ? '' : undefined}` sites to `inert={readOnly || undefined}` so React passes a boolean true that it now emits as a real HTML attribute. Optional defense-in-depth: also guard the reducer in dspStore.js (e.g., wrap updateOutput/toggleRoute/setMaster/etc. with `if (readOnly) return state;` checks).
